inputs.prompt: Newsletter: The Margin. Tone: Sharp, analytical, sardonic. Audience: Finance professionals. Edition summary: This week's main piece examines new data showing that companies that announced mass layoffs in 2024 saw their stock price rise an average of 7% within 30 days — but revenue declined 4% within two quarters. The market rewards cutting people and punishes the consequences. No emoji.
Newsletter: Weekend Reader. Tone: Warm, bookish. Edition summary: Five book recommendations for the weekend. Emoji: OK.

Newsletter: EU Policy Pulse. Tone: Authoritative, measured, policy-focused. Audience: Brussels-based policy professionals. Edition summary: The European Parliament's surprise rejection of the proposed Digital Infrastructure Act, a law three years in the making, and what this means for the Commission's digital strategy heading into 2027. No emoji. Previous high-performer: 'The regulation nobody read (until it failed).'

Newsletter: After. Tone: Gentle, honest, no clichés. Audience: Recently bereaved people. Edition summary: This edition covers the financial logistics nobody tells you about after a death — closing bank accounts, dealing with recurring subscriptions, the spouse's tax situation. Practical, not emotional — the readers are already emotional. No emoji.

Newsletter: Supply Chain Quarterly. Tone: Professional, dry-ish. Audience: Logistics managers. Edition summary: Updated ISO 28000 compliance requirements for third-party warehousing partners, with a comparison table of the 2019 vs 2025 standard. This is genuinely important for readers but sounds deathly boring — the subject line needs to make them open it anyway. No emoji.

Newsletter: The Morning Brief. Tone: Friendly, not desperate. Audience: Lapsed subscribers who haven't opened in 60+ days. Edition summary: This is a re-engagement email, not a regular edition. The reader needs a reason to come back. Reference what they've missed (3 editions that got highest engagement) without guilt-tripping. Emoji: One max.

Newsletter: Afrotech Digest. Tone: Optimistic, insider. Audience: African tech founders and investors. Edition summary: Nigeria's fintech licensing crackdown — 14 companies lost their licences this week. Analysis of what the Central Bank is actually targeting (hint: it's not the companies making headlines). Plus: Kenya's first AI unicorn, and why South African VCs are moving to Lagos. Emoji: OK.

inputs.description: Contradictory signals — good and bad news
inputs.prompt: Newsletter: Climate Monitor. Tone: Evidence-based, neither alarmist nor complacent. Audience: Climate scientists and policy professionals. Edition summary: Two stories that directly contradict each other — global renewable energy investment hit a record $1.7 trillion, AND global CO2 emissions also hit a new record. The subject line must acknowledge both without false balance. No emoji.
